elements.text: (ii) The maximum coverage amount available from NFIP per unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: The condominium owners association must maintain contents coverage on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: building for the lower of (i) the actual cash value of the contents in the building that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are owned in common by the association members or (ii) the maximum amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: contents coverage sold by the NFIP for a condominium building.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The deductible of the condominium owners association’s coverage may not exceed
elements.label: text

elements.text: the maximum deductible amount currently allowed under the NFIP for condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: association building coverage. The deductible for association building contents may
elements.label: text

elements.text: not exceed the maximum deductible amount currently allowed under the NFIP for
elements.label: text

elements.text: association building contents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B. Unit owner’s coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: To the extent the condominium owners association’s building coverage meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum requirements above, but the Condominium Unit allocation does not meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 1- to 4-unit coverage requirement, the Borrower must maintain supplemental
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage on the Condominium Unit. The coverage must be at least equal to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: difference between the condominium associations’ building coverage allocated to that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit and the amount required on a 1- to 4-unit property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The deductible may not exceed the maximum deductible allowed for a 1- to 4-unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Cooperative Corporations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Corporation must maintain flood insurance on each building that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located in an SFHA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The building coverage must be the lower of (i) 100% of the building’s replacement cost,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or (ii) the maximum amount of building coverage sold by the NFIP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The contents coverage must be the lower of (i) 100% of the insurable replacement cost of
elements.label: text

elements.text: all contents owned by the Cooperative Corporation, or (ii) the maximum amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: contents coverage sold by the NFIP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The deductible of the Cooperative Corporation coverage may not exceed the maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: deductible amount currently allowed under NFIP for building coverage. The deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Cooperative Corporation building contents may not exceed the maximum deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount currently allowed under the NFIP for contents coverage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A blanket policy of flood insurance covering more than one building or structure in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project is acceptable if each building or structure covered by the blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy is insured for at least the individual building and contents limits stated above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Requirements during an NFIP lapse
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the NFIP’s authority to issue new and renewal policies and increase coverage on existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies lapses, Freddie Mac requirements for flood insurance remain unchanged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must ensure payments to renew expiring policies are made as scheduled.
elements.label: text

elements.text: During a lapse, if a Borrower applies for NFIP flood insurance, acceptable evidence pending
elements.label: text

elements.text: issuance of a final NFIP policy may include one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed and executed NFIP Flood Insurance Application plus a copy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s premium check or agent’s paid receipt,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed and executed NFIP Flood Insurance Application plus the final
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement reflecting the flood insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: collected at closing, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed and executed NFIP General Change Endorsement Form showing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment of the current flood insurance policy by the property seller to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers that accept interim evidence of NFIP coverage as described above during a
elements.label: text

elements.text: lapse in the NFIP’s authority must confirm that they have final evidence of coverage meeting
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of Section 4703.3 once the NFIP’s authority has been reinstated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Private flood insurance is not affected by a lapse in the NFIP’s authority.
elements.label: text

elements.text: association or corporation.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project or Cooperative Project.

elements.label: text

elements.text: maintains separate accounts for the operating budget and the reserve fund. The depository

elements.text: directly to the association or corporation.

elements.label: text

elements.text: association, Cooperative Corporation or other community association using the management
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm’s services. The management firm does not have the authority to draw checks on or to

elements.text: transfer funds from the reserve fund of the condominium homeowners association or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation.

elements.text: 4703.6: Mortgage clause (07/09/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Effective June 10, 2020, the content of this section has moved from Section 8202.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: All policies documenting insurance coverage(s) obtained in accordance with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for 1- to 4-unit properties must have the insurance industry’s standard mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: clause. Such clause must provide that the insurer will notify the named mortgagee at least 10
elements.label: text

elements.text: days before cancelation of the policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is owned by Freddie Mac, “(name of Seller/Servicer), its successors and assigns”
elements.label: text

elements.text: should be named as mortgagee instead of Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation. In deed-of￾trust jurisdictions, the mortgagee should be designated as “(name of Seller/Servicer), its
elements.label: text

elements.text: successors and assigns, beneficiary.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation must be named as mortgagee, the endorsement
elements.label: text

elements.text: must show the Seller’s address in lieu of Freddie Mac’s, as shown in the example below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: FEDERAL HOME LOAN MORTGAGE CORPORATION
elements.label: text

elements.text: C/O ABC SAVINGS AND LOAN ASSOCIATION
elements.label: text

elements.text: 100 MAIN STREET
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOMETOWN USA 12345
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is registered with MERS® and is originated naming MERS as the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee of record, under no circumstances may MERS be named as loss payee on any
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of how the mortgage clause is endorsed, or if the Mortgage is registered with MERS,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer must arrange for all insurance drafts, notices, policies, invoices, etc. to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivered directly to the Seller/Servicer. Although the MERS address appears in county land
elements.label: text

elements.text: records, the address for MERS must not be given to organizations that normally direct mail to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer or subservicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a mortgage clause is not applicable (e.g., in a separate policy of commercial general
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability), a certificate of insurance must be provided to the Seller/Servicer. This certificate must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain the information required for certificates or other evidence of insurance in Section 8202.4,
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Seller/Servicer named as certificate holder instead of mortgagee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4703.7: Localized perils insurance (06/10/20)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer becomes aware of localized perils (i.e., sinkhole, mine subsidence, volcanic
elements.label: text

elements.text: eruption, landslides) that are not covered by standard property insurance, then the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must require the Borrower to obtain appropriate insurance coverage in accordance with the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Security Instrument and applicable law.

elements.text: Chapter 5101: Using Loan Product Advisor®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5101.1: General information for using Loan Product
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Product Advisor® overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note Date references
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Key Number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Number of Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data accuracy and compliance with other Loan Product Advisor requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan Product Advisor overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor is Freddie Mac’s automated loan assessment system that indicates
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether a Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac and whether the Seller is eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief from certain representations and warranties, subject to the requirements of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Note Date references
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this Chapter 5101, all references to the Note Date also refer to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification date for Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages that are Home Possible®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, or the Effective Date of Permanent Financing for Construction to Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage qualifies as a Loan Product Advisor Mortgage when all of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was submitted to Loan Product Advisor for the first time no later than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The last submission to Loan Product Advisor is no more than 120 days before the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date and no later than the Note Date except as otherwise permitted in Section 5101.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for resubmissions after the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage received Risk Class of Accept or Caution on the Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on the last submission to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage complies with all the requirements of this Chapter 5101, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery of the Key Number in ULDD Data Point Automated Underwriting Case
elements.label: text

elements.text: Identifier
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage is a Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgage if any of the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage never was submitted to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was submitted to Loan Product Advisor and did not receive a Risk Class
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Accept or Caution
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was submitted to Loan Product Advisor for the first time after the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was submitted to Loan Product Advisor but did not comply with this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5101
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was submitted to Loan Product Advisor but was not eligible to be a Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor Mortgage under the Guide or the Seller’s other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was submitted to Loan Product Advisor, but was delivered without the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Key Number
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor assesses data and returns a Feedback Certificate with the results of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor submission.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The messages reflected on the Feedback Certificate are for guidance purposes only. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: messages are not part of, and are not a replacement or substitute for, the requirements in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents, unless specifically stated in the Guide or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller’s other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor Mortgages, the Seller must retain the Last Feedback Certificate in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Documentation Level shown on the Last Feedback Certificate indicates the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of documentation acceptable for the Mortgage (i.e., Streamlined Accept Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Standard Documentation).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 5501 for asset documentation requirements using Streamlined Accept
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Standard Documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must have processes in place to ensure that the requirements on the Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate and other applicable requirements of the Guide and the Seller’s other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents are met. If the Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac does not comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Last Feedback Certificate, the Guide or the Seller’s other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, Freddie Mac may require additional documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Key Number
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Key Number is a unique identifier assigned to a Mortgage application by Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor when the application is first submitted to Loan Product Advisor. The Key Number is
elements.label: text

elements.text: returned to the Seller on the Feedback Certificate and is used by the Seller and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: to identify an individual Mortgage (e.g., a purchase or refinance) from application through
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing and delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Key Number is only valid for use with one Mortgage. The Key Number from a previously
elements.label: text

elements.text: closed Mortgage may not be re-used to process or originate another Mortgage. Additionally,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the Borrower has more than one Mortgage in process, each loan must have a separate
elements.label: text

elements.text: application and a different Key Number.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5102.1: Underwriting a Mortgage (02/04/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview of credit reputation, capacity and collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation requirements for creditworthiness
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Number of Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview of credit reputation, capacity and collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: When underwriting a Mortgage, the Seller must determine, in compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in the Guide and Purchase Documents, that the Borrower is creditworthy (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable credit reputation and capacity) and the Mortgaged Premises (i.e., collateral) is
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate for the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit reputation, capacity and collateral are often called the “three Cs” of underwriting.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When one of these components does not comply with the Guide requirements or if there is
elements.label: text

elements.text: excessive layering of risk across components, the Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documentation requirements for creditworthiness
elements.label: text

elements.text: All information used to evaluate the creditworthiness of the Borrower must be supported by
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documentation must be obtained before, as applicable, the Note Date or:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages, the Effective Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Permanent Financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages, the modification date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages, the Conversion Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The date of the applicable assumption agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Number of Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not limit the number of Borrowers on the Mortgage or require they be
elements.label: text

elements.text: related.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The overall creditworthiness of each Borrower, and all Borrowers collectively, must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The presence of more than one Borrower helps to reduce risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5102.2: Methods of underwriting (03/01/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: In many cases, submitting the Mortgage to Loan Product Advisor is the most effective way to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assess Borrower creditworthiness and identify excessive layering of risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor assesses the Mortgage based on the data submitted to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor and provided by the consumer reporting agencies and returns a Risk Class of either
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accept or Caution on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Chapter 5101 for additional requirements for Loan Product Advisor Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Accept Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with a Risk Class of Accept on the Last Feedback Certificate, the Seller is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for relief from representations and warranties for creditworthiness and layering of
elements.label: text

elements.text: risk when the requirements in Section 5101.2(a) are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Caution Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with a Risk Class of Caution on the Last Feedback Certificate to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, the Seller must manually underwrite the Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 5102.2(b) below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Manually Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manually Underwritten Mortgages are those for which the Seller evaluates the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and data, makes the final determination regarding Borrower creditworthiness and
elements.label: text

elements.text: layering of risk and complies with the requirements of this Section 5102.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, manual underwriting is required for all Mortgages that
elements.label: text

elements.text: did not receive a Risk Class of Accept on the Last Feedback Certificate, including Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: never submitted to Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower creditworthiness
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s conclusion that the Borrower is creditworthy and has the ability to meet all
elements.label: text

elements.text: current obligations, including the new Mortgage, must be based on the documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the Mortgage file and described on Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transmittal Summary, or another document in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To determine that the Borrower is creditworthy, the Seller must manually underwrite the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage as required in Topics 5100 through 5500 for the Mortgage to be eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine that the Borrower has an acceptable credit reputation in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with requirements in Chapter 5202 and confirm that the Mortgage meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum Indicator Score requirements, if applicable, stated in Exhibit 25, Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Risk Class and/or Minimum Indicator Score Requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine the Borrower’s capacity to repay the Mortgage and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly obligations by analyzing file documentation for the following factors:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Stable monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Freddie Mac Income Calculator may be used to assess the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: stable monthly income and will determine whether the Seller is eligible for relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: from enforcement of certain income representations and warranties. The results are
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected on the Freddie Mac Income Calculator Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Freddie Mac Income Calculator requirements, see the following Guide sections:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income calculator requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: section(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated income assessment for employed income Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated income assessment for self-employed income Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated income assessment for rental income from the subject 2- to
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4-unit Primary Residence or non-subject investment property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly debt payment-to-income (DTI) ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information about how the Borrower has paid obligations in the past
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages, the Seller must presume the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity to repay is not acceptable when all of the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transaction is a cash-out refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The monthly DTI ratio exceeds 42%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any Borrower has an Underwriting Score less than 700
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total loan-to-value (TLTV) ratio is greater than 75%
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower who increases debt and then periodically uses refinance or debt consolidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: to reduce payments to a manageable level presents a higher degree of risk. The Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: should consider the Borrower’s short- and long-term capacity to repay the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Layering of risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all Manually Underwritten Mortgages, the Seller is responsible for determining that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac by performing an assessment of layering
elements.label: text

elements.text: of risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Multiple characteristics that increase risk without sufficient risk-offsetting factors are
elements.label: text

elements.text: likely to result in excessive risk layering.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An offsetting factor does not need to be established for each risk factor if the overall risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: is balanced. However, when multiple risk factors are present, more conservative
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting is required to assess if the Mortgage is acceptable for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgage characteristics may introduce an additional layer of risk that
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be considered in evaluating capacity:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payoff of a junior lien from the proceeds of a refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A cash-out refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Borrower with low or no reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine that each component (credit reputation, capacity and
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral) is acceptable and that the overall layering of risk is not excessive. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusion that the Mortgage is acceptable cannot be reached by looking only at a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting component or by placing the most weight on a single component but may
elements.label: text

elements.text: result from balancing the weakness of one component against the strength of the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: two components.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A Mortgage where the Borrower has weak capacity may be found to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable because of strong collateral and credit reputation, but a Mortgage where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has weak capacity and weak credit reputation is not acceptable because only
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral is strong.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even when each of the three components is acceptable, layered risk may make a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage unacceptable. Mortgage characteristics (e.g., Mortgage Product, purpose of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, property type) may add layers of risk that must be considered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table provides examples of Mortgage characteristics that increase risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This list does not identify all possible risk factors or combinations of risks for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, nor is it intended to imply that an individual characteristic is unacceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: horizontally, it shows how risk may be layered across components.

elements.text: The Seller’s analysis of layering of risk must be documented in the Mortgage file and at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The identified risk factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The identified risk offsetting factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of the risk offsetting factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A written conclusion that the Mortgage does not exhibit excessive layering of risks
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Additional requirements for Caution Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all Caution Mortgages, there is a strong indication that the layering of risk is excessive
elements.label: text

elements.text: and that acceptability and compliance with Freddie Mac requirements is unlikely.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must analyze all risk factors present in the Mortgage file, including those
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified in the Feedback Certificate, and document satisfactory offsetting factors in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file to ensure that the Mortgage is acceptable. The offsets used must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: information not considered by Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may not use information already considered by Loan Product Advisor to

elements.text: an energy-efficient property (see Section 5401.1), may be used by the Seller in making a
elements.label: text

elements.text: case that capacity is acceptable.

elements.label: text

elements.text: risk is not present because Loan Product Advisor has already considered them:

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The level of reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The type of Mortgage Product and/or offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The type of property securing the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FICO® score, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any combination of these factors

elements.text: When a Mortgage includes a non-occupying Borrower, all of the following apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a purchase or “no cash-out” refinance transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The non-occupying Borrower must not be an interested party to the transaction (for example,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the builder, property seller, real estate agent or broker)

elements.text: ■ The funds used to qualify for the Mortgage may come from the occupant and/or the non￾occupant Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements for specific underwriting methods also apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Accept Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The loan-to-value (LTV) ratio must not exceed 95%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Calculation or evaluation of the occupant Borrower’s monthly housing expense-to￾income ratio or the occupant Borrower’s monthly debt payment-to-income ratio is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Manually Underwritten Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The LTV ratio must not exceed 90%
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 35% of their stable monthly income and/or qualifying asset amount as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5307.1(b), and the occupant Borrower’s monthly debt payment-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not exceed 43% of their stable monthly income and/or qualifying asset amount as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5307.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or an individual(s) who has a disability and the Borrower is their parent or legal guardian. Refer

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Land Trust Mortgages under the terms of this section and all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Location of Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises must be located in a State that recognizes and permits the use of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Land Trusts by statute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Beneficiary of a Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one beneficiary of the Land Trust must be a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each beneficiary who is a Borrower must be fully underwritten and a qualified Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the beneficiary’s individual capacity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A beneficiary of the Land Trust Mortgage who is a Borrower must be deemed to be an
elements.label: text

elements.text: owner of the property/Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Trust requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: All beneficiaries of the Land Trust must be individuals.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The trustee of the Land Trust must be a corporation or financial institution customarily
elements.label: text

elements.text: engaged in the business of acting as trustee for land trusts in the applicable jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises must be the only asset of the Land Trust.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Property type and occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Land Trust Mortgage must be secured by one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 1- to 4-unit Primary Residence occupied by a beneficiary of the Land Trust who is
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A second home occupied for some portion of the year by a beneficiary of the Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust who is a Borrower, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 1- to 4-unit Investment Property, provided at least one beneficiary of the Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust is a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Required Uniform Instrument signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Form of signature for Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each beneficiary of a Land Trust who is a Borrower must sign the Note in their
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual capacity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Signature required on Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: The trustee of the Land Trust must execute the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 4101.6 for additional Note and Security Instrument signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Special documentation: Collateral Assignment of Beneficial Interest (“Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Collateral Assignment requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each beneficiary of the Land Trust who is a Borrower must execute a Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment under which the beneficiary:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Grants the lender named in the Security Instrument and the Note and the lender’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: successors and assigns (the “Lender”) a security interest in the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: (collectively, the “Collateral”):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All of the beneficiary’s rights, title, powers and interests in, under and to the Land
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The property held in the Land Trust (including any rights to earnings or proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: from that property), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Land Trust agreement (including any powers of direction or control over the
elements.label: text

elements.text: trustee of the Land Trust or the property)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certifies that no prior security interest in the Collateral has been granted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Agrees not to make any further assignment or take any other action by which all or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any part of the Collateral is transferred in any way without the written approval of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lender; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Agrees that the Collateral Assignment is for collateral security only, no liability under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Land Trust agreement accrues to the Lender by virtue of the Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment, and signing the Collateral Agreement does not relieve the beneficiary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any responsibility or liability under the Land Trust agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Collateral Assignment signatures requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Collateral Assignment must be signed by the appropriate parties as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each beneficiary of the Land Trust who is a Borrower must individually execute the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Collateral Assignment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lender named in the Security Instrument and the Note in whose favor the
elements.label: text

elements.text: beneficiary of the Land Trust grants a security interest under the Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment (the “Assignee”) must accept the Collateral Assignment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The trustee of the Land Trust must receive and agree to, endorse and/or acknowledge,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as appropriate in the applicable jurisdiction, the Collateral Assignment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Collateral Assignment form
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Sample Illinois Collateral Assignment form
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 50, Collateral Assignment of Beneficial Interest in Land Trust, is a sample form
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Collateral Assignment for Land Trust Mortgages secured by Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: located in Illinois. The Seller may use:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s form for Land Trust Mortgages secured by Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: located in Illinois, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An alternative form that meets Illinois requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Collateral Assignment forms for use in other jurisdictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Land Trust Mortgages secured by property located in jurisdictions other than
elements.label: text

elements.text: Illinois, the Seller must use a form of Collateral Assignment that reflects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the jurisdiction in which the Mortgaged Premises is located (unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller determines that the use of Form 50 is appropriate for the specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Collateral Assignment representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that the Collateral Assignment is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appropriately executed by each beneficiary of the Land Trust who is a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accepted by the lender named in the Security Instrument and the Note in whose favor
elements.label: text

elements.text: the beneficiary of the Land Trust grants a security interest under the Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment (the “Assignee”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appropriately received and agreed to, endorsed and/or acknowledged, as appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the applicable jurisdiction, by the trustee of the Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Valid, enforceable and meets the provisions of the applicable jurisdiction, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: relevant statutes, regulations and judicial decisions if the Land Trust Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by Mortgaged Premises located in a jurisdiction other than Illinois or by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises located in Illinois and the Seller uses a form of Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment other than Form 50.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Title and title insurance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Land Trust Mortgage must constitute a valid First Lien.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Land Trust Mortgage must be covered by a title insurance policy that meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Chapter 4702.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Mortgage file documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to other requirements in the Purchase Documents, for Land Trust Mortgages, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file also must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copy of Land Trust agreement: A copy of the original Land Trust agreement or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: abstract if required by the jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of power of direction: Documentation evidencing that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: beneficiary(ies) of the Land Trust holding the power of direction as provided in the trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents have authorized and directed the trustee of the trust to execute the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents. If the trust documents require more than one beneficiary holding the power of
elements.label: text

elements.text: direction to so authorize and direct the trustee, then the documentation must evidence that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requisite number of beneficiaries have so directed the trustee; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Collateral Assignment: A fully executed or certified copy of Collateral Assignment, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described below for the applicable jurisdiction. A “Fully Executed Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment” is the original Collateral Assignment executed by each beneficiary of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Land Trust who is a Borrower in that beneficiary’s individual capacity, accepted by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender named in the Security Instrument and the Note in whose favor the beneficiary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Land Trust grants a security interest under the Collateral Assignment (the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Assignee”), and received and agreed to or endorsed and/or acknowledged by the trustee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A “Certified Copy of Collateral Assignment” is a copy, certified by the trustee as a true
elements.label: text

elements.text: and complete copy, of the Fully Executed Collateral Assignment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgaged Premises located in Illinois: For Mortgaged Premises located in Illinois,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file also must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certified Copy of Collateral Assignment: A Certified Copy of Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment. The Seller must ensure that the Fully Executed Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment can be obtained from the trustee upon request by a Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Facsimile Assignment of Beneficial Interest:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Facsimile Assignment of Beneficial Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: If… Then…
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An Illinois jurisdiction requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: recording of a facsimile
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignment of beneficial
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest (or similar document)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“facsimile”), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Until the trustee is provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the recorded facsimile, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: trustee will not receive and
elements.label: text

elements.text: agree to and/or endorse and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: acknowledge the Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment executed by each
elements.label: text

elements.text: beneficiary of the Land Trust
elements.label: text

elements.text: who is a Borrower in that
elements.label: text

elements.text: beneficiary’s individual
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity and accepted by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller may retain in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file copies of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed and accepted Collateral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assignment and the facsimile sent
elements.label: text

elements.text: for recording, provided that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller also retains in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file a letter of intent confirming
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Seller will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Obtain the trustee receipt and
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement/endorsement/
elements.label: text

elements.text: acknowledgement upon

elements.text: receipt of the recorded

elements.text: facsimile, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Place the Certified Copy of

elements.text: Collateral Assignment in the

elements.text: ❑ Mortgaged Premises located in a State other than Illinois: For Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises located in any other State that by statute recognizes and permits the use of

elements.text: Land Trusts, the Fully Executed Collateral Assignment is required

elements.text: Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assessing Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept Mortgages with Borrowers who do not have a usable Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inquiries on a Borrower credit report
elements.label: text
