elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower cash flow included in the Loan Product Advisor assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Documenting Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: To document the credit reputation for Loan Product Advisor Accept Mortgages, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must use the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Uniform Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The credit reports (see Section 5203.1 for details)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Last Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An underwriting summary (e.g. Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Summary)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Direct verification of Mortgage debt, rental payments and other debts not shown on the credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports are typically not required for Accept Mortgages. For Accept Mortgages with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers who do not have a usable Credit Score, the requirements in Section 5201.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: below apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is not required to obtain or document an explanation of adverse or derogatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit information on Accept Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Assessing Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Accept Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor has assessed the Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and determined that the credit reputation is acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Accept Mortgages with Borrowers who do not have a usable Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgages where not all Borrowers have a usable Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages where not all Borrowers have a usable Credit Score, Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor will apply the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one Borrower must have a usable Credit Score, as determined by Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transaction must be a purchase or “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must be a 1-unit property, occupied by all Borrowers as
elements.label: text

elements.text: their Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages other than Refi Possible® Mortgages, if the Borrower(s) without a usable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score contributes 50% or more of the total monthly income, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine that the following additional requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Borrower without a usable Credit Score must have at least two payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: references in the United States comprised of Noncredit Payment References and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tradelines not appearing on the credit report that meet the requirements in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Guide sections:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Written verifications 5102.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Age of documentation 5102.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation of Tradelines and Noncredit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: If two or more Borrowers without a usable Credit Score have the same payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: reference, then the payment reference may count for each of those Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each payment reference must have existed for at least the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one Borrower without a usable Credit Score must have a housing payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history as one of the payment references and all housing payment histories must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: no 30-day or greater Delinquencies in the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event more than one Borrower without a usable Credit Score has a housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment history, then all such housing payment histories for the most recent 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months (or length of housing payment history if less than 12 months) must be verified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For all payment references other than housing, in the most recent 12 months, there
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No more than one 30-day Delinquency; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No 60-day or greater Delinquencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Borrower without a usable Credit Score must have no collections (other than
elements.label: text

elements.text: medical), judgments or tax liens filed in the most recent 24 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgages where no Borrower has a Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages where no Borrower has a Credit Score, Loan Product Advisor will apply
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transaction must be a purchase or “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must be a 1-unit property, occupied by all Borrowers as
elements.label: text

elements.text: their Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: TLTV (HTLTV) ratios must not exceed 95%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a fixed-rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must not be a super conforming Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must not be a Refi Possible Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, unless the Last Feedback Certificate includes a feedback message that
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicates positive Borrower cash flow was identified, the Seller must determine that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Borrower must have at least two payment references in the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: comprised of Noncredit Payment References and/or Tradelines not appearing on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report that meet the requirements in the following Guide sections:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Written verifications Section 5102.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Age of documentation Section 5102.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation of Tradelines and Noncredit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5202.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If two or more Borrowers have the same payment reference, then the payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: reference may count for each of those Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each payment reference must have existed for at least the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least one Borrower must have a housing payment history as one of the payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: references and all housing payment histories must have no 30-day or greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquencies in the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event more than one Borrower has a housing payment history, then all such
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing payment histories for the most recent 12 months (or length of housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment history if less than 12 months) must be verified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For all payment references other than housing, in the most recent 12 months, there
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No more than one 30-day Delinquency; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ No 60-day or greater Delinquencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each Borrower must have no collections (other than medical), judgments or tax liens
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed in the most recent 24 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the credit reputation for all Borrowers is established using only Noncredit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment References, at least one Borrower must participate in a homeownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: education program before the Note Date, or the Effective Date of Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages. (See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5103.4 for requirements related to homeownership education.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Inquiries on a Borrower credit report
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the credit report indicates that a creditor has made an inquiry within the previous 90-
elements.label: text

elements.text: day period, the Seller must determine if additional credit was granted. If additional credit was
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain verification of the debt; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include it when qualifying the Borrower (subject to the requirements in Section 5401.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the monthly debt payment-to-income ratio)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Rent payment history included in the Loan Product Advisor assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i)Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certain Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor’s assessment of a Borrower’s rent payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history submitted to Loan Product Advisor may positively impact the credit assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any such Mortgage, the Feedback Certificate will return a feedback message
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicating the Borrower’s rent payment history was included in the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor to consider a Borrower’s rent payment history in the credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment, the following requirements must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Mortgage must be a purchase transaction Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. At least one Borrower with a rent payment history must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a usable Credit Score, as determined by Loan Product Advisor,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a First-Time Homebuyer who intends to occupy the subject property as their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have been renting for a minimum of 12 months with a monthly rent payment of at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least $300 paid from the depository account(s) on the verification report or
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented as described in Section 5201.1(e)(iii)(B) below
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller must submit to Loan Product Advisor the current monthly rent amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid by the Borrower and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain a verification report of the depository account(s) from which the rent
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments are made, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide true for the Positive Rental History Indicator
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Verification reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each verification report obtained, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that each depository account is owned by the Borrower and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: account(s) is the one from which the Borrower pays rent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the most current version of the verification report is used by Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor. If the Seller obtains an updated report, the Mortgage must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: resubmitted to Loan Product Advisor to ensure assessment of the most current
elements.label: text

elements.text: information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain the verification report in the Mortgage file for transactions where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Last Feedback Certificate includes a feedback message that the rent payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history was successfully identified in Loan Product Advisor, resulting in a Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: Class of Accept
elements.label: text

elements.text: The expiration date of the verification report reflected in feedback messaging
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the age of documentation requirement in Section 5102.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Borrower provided documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval before selling Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: using Borrower provided documentation to support the Borrower’s rent payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history to Freddie Mac by contacting its Freddie Mac representative or Customer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller indicates positive rental history in the Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission and the Last Feedback Certificate indicates positive rent payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: was included in the credit assessment using Borrower provided documentation, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must maintain the following documentation in the Mortgage file supporting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly rent amount submitted to Loan Product Advisor:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Executed lease(s) for the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Proof of timely rent payments (i.e., no 30-day or greater late payments) for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: full lease amount paid by the Borrower in accordance with the terms of the lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the most recent 12 months as of the Application Received Date. Such
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments must be documented by one or more of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Canceled checks
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Asset account statements as described in Section 5501.1(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third-party asset verifications as described in Section 5501.1(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verification reports as described in Section 5201.1(e)(iii)(A) above
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence of rent payments transferred from the Borrower’s bank account
elements.label: text

elements.text: through a third-party money transfer application
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Verification of rent and/or evidence of cash payments (e.g., money orders)
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not acceptable documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documentation used to support the Borrower’s rent payment history must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the age of documentation requirement in Section 5102.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Borrower cash flow included in the Loan Product Advisor assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certain Mortgages, Loan Product Advisor’s assessment of a Borrower’s monthly cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: flow identified on a verification report and submitted to Loan Product Advisor may
elements.label: text

elements.text: positively impact the credit assessment. For any such Mortgage, the Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: will return a feedback message indicating the Borrower’s positive cash flow was included
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor to consider Borrower cash flow in the credit assessment, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must obtain a verification report of the Borrower’s checking, savings, investment
elements.label: text

elements.text: and retirement account(s) with at least 12 months of data and submit to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each verification report obtained, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that each asset in the verification report is owned by at least one Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: and such Borrower has access to the funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the most current version of the verification report is used by Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor. If the Seller obtains an updated report, the Mortgage must be resubmitted to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor to ensure assessment of the most current information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain the verification report in the Mortgage file if the Last Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes a feedback message that positive Borrower cash flow was identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: The expiration date of the verification report is reflected in feedback messaging complies
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the age of documentation requirements in Section 5102.4.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5202: Credit Assessment for Manually
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Underwritten Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5202.1: Credit assessment for Manually Underwritten
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establishing Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluating Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adverse or derogatory credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluating other credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting and delivering Underwriting Scores and Indicator Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manually Underwritten Mortgages must meet the requirements of this chapter and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Establishing Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: To establish an acceptable credit reputation, there must be at least a minimum amount of credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: history available as described below. There may be situations in which a credit history of short
elements.label: text

elements.text: duration is sufficient to demonstrate an acceptable reputation. A credit history of short duration
elements.label: text

elements.text: adds a layer of risk that must be considered in evaluating the Borrower’s credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Minimum number of payment references
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least one Borrower whose income or assets are used for qualification must have a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum number of payment references consisting of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least three Tradelines, whether or not on the credit report, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a Borrower does not have three Tradelines, at least four Noncredit Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a total of four Tradelines and Noncredit Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Tradeline for an account for which the Borrower is not the primary account holder but is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized user may be considered a Borrower’s Tradeline if the Seller maintains in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file documentation that evidences at least one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Another Borrower on the Mortgage owns the Tradeline in question
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Tradeline is owned by the Borrower’s spouse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has been making the payments on the account for the last 12 months and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the monthly payment reported on the Borrower’s credit report is included in the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: debt payment-to-income (DTI) ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a permanent or nonpermanent resident alien Borrower has established the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of payment references required above in the United States, a U.S. credit history may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be supplemented with a credit history from a foreign country to establish an acceptable credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation from a foreign country must meet all applicable Guide documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional Guide provisions related to foreign documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements Guide section
elements.label: text

elements.text: General requirements for verification documents Section 5102.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Age of documentation Section 5102.4
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit reports Section 5203.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Noncredit Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Noncredit Payment Reference must have existed for at least 12 months to be used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish a minimum payment history. A Borrower with a credit reputation established using
elements.label: text

elements.text: only Noncredit Payment References adds an additional layer of risk that must be considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Seller. In addition, when the credit reputation for all Borrowers is established using
elements.label: text

elements.text: only Noncredit Payment References, at least one Borrower must participate in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeownership education program before the Note Date or the Effective Date of Permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Financing for Construction to Permanent Mortgages and Renovation Mortgages. See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5103.4 for requirements related to homeownership education.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Borrower has a housing payment history, the Seller must verify both current and
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior housing payment histories for no less than the most recent 12 months (or length of
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing payment history if less than 12 months) in accordance with Section 5202.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documented payments of a voluntary nature, such as deposits to a savings account,
elements.label: text

elements.text: contributions to a payroll savings plan or contributions to a stock purchase plan of at least 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months, may be included as one of the Noncredit Payment References if the history shows
elements.label: text

elements.text: periodic deposits (at least quarterly) resulting in a growing balance over the year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documenting Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Required documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is required to document the Borrower’s credit reputation and the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination that the Borrower’s credit reputation is acceptable. Although the documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to establish an acceptable credit reputation may vary, the documentation in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file must clearly demonstrate the Borrower met his or her obligations over an
elements.label: text

elements.text: extended period of time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To document the Borrower’s credit reputation, the Seller must use the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The credit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Caution Mortgages, the Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any direct verification of payment histories
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An underwriting summary such as Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Summary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional documentation may be required depending on the Borrower’s credit history
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must first document the Borrower’s existing credit history by documenting a
elements.label: text

elements.text: history of payments made by the Borrower through the credit reporting process as detailed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5203.1 and/or by direct verification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the credit report does not include a reference for each significant open debt, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing debt, listed on the Mortgage application, the Seller must obtain a separate written
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification for each unreported debt. Accounts listed on the credit report as “will rate by mail
elements.label: text

elements.text: only” or “need written authorization” also require separate verification by the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must document in the Mortgage file and describe on Form 1077 or another
elements.label: text

elements.text: document in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any additional risks uncovered during the evaluation of the credit history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller’s analysis and conclusion that the Borrower’s credit reputation is acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documents used to establish the Borrower’s credit history must be consistent with each
elements.label: text

elements.text: other and with the Borrower information found on the Mortgage application. The Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file must also include any supporting documentation necessary to address derogatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: information or other risks identified by other sources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When underwriting with Credit Scores, the Seller must identify on Form 1077, or another
elements.label: text

elements.text: document in the Mortgage file, the Credit Score selected for each Borrower and the process
elements.label: text

elements.text: used to select that Credit Score from among all Credit Scores received for that Borrower. An
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation for any Credit Scores found to be unusable due to an insufficient number of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tradelines or inaccurate information should also be included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Documentation of Tradelines and Noncredit Payment References
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tradelines and Noncredit Payment References may appear on a credit report meeting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report requirements of Section 5203.1, or the Seller may obtain a direct verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the creditor or other acceptable documentation of payment history meeting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the verification of a Noncredit Payment Reference on the credit report or direct verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: is provided by a source other than a professional business, the Mortgage file must also include
elements.label: text

elements.text: other documentation supporting the payment history (e.g., canceled checks, depository
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements, documentation from a third-party money transfer application or service or receipts
elements.label: text

elements.text: issued by the creditor), subject to requirements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Direct verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: A direct verification may be either a completed verification form or a computer-generated
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment history obtained by the Seller directly from the creditor and signed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual providing the verification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Direct verifications must contain sufficient information to establish all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name and address of the creditor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name of the payor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name and title of the individual providing the credit reference
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The telephone number of the creditor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The account number, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The nature of the obligation (e.g., rent, utilities, payment for purchases, insurance)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The highest credit balance, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the payment due
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The outstanding balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current and historical status of the account. The completed verification form must
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicate the number of times and duration of times past due. The historical account
elements.label: text

elements.text: status format should be “0 x 30, 0 x 60, 0 x 90 days” late. However, alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: formats are acceptable as long as the meaning is clear. Statements such as “current,”
elements.label: text

elements.text: “as agreed” or “satisfactory” are not acceptable by themselves because they are too
elements.label: text

elements.text: vague.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a housing payment history, age of the reference if the length of housing payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history is less than 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: General reference letters without the above information are not sufficient documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: for establishing an acceptable credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Other acceptable documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of a direct verification from the creditor, the Seller may obtain canceled checks,
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipts issued by the creditor in accordance with the requirements of Section 5202.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: below, documentation from a third-party money transfer application or service or
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository account statements from the Borrower. The Mortgage file must also contain
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation that substantiates the terms of the debt repayment (e.g., a copy of a fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed lease agreement for a rental verification).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The obtained documentation must contain sufficient information to establish all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name and address of the creditor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The name of the payor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The nature of the obligation (e.g., rent, utilities, payment for purchases, insurance)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The highest credit balance, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the payment due
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The outstanding balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current and historical status of the account, including number of times past due
elements.label: text

elements.text: and how many days past due
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a housing payment history, age of the reference if the length of housing payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: history is less than 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documented payments of a voluntary nature, such as deposits to a savings account,
elements.label: text

elements.text: contributions to a payroll savings plan or contributions to a stock purchase plan of at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12 months may be included as one of the Noncredit Payment References if the history
elements.label: text

elements.text: shows periodic deposits (at least quarterly) resulting in a growing balance over the year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a savings history is used as one of the Noncredit Payment References, the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: must contain documentation, such as depository account statements, that meet the asset
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility and documentation requirements of Section 5501.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For rental verification, in order to meet the documentation requirements of this subsection,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain a copy of a fully executed lease agreement and either canceled checks,
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository account statements, documentation from a third-party money transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: application service or receipts from the creditor for payments in cash in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements of this Guide chapter, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm that the Borrower has made timely payments in accordance with the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lease for the most recent 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Requirements for receipts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Receipts from the creditor are acceptable documentation only if the payments being
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified were made in cash and there is no evidence in the Mortgage file that the payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: were made by checks or direct bank transfers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Evaluating Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s credit reputation must be evaluated by one of the following methods:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Borrowers with usable Credit Scores, manually underwriting with FICO® scores and
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason codes and analyzing the information in each of the Borrower’s credit reports and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: verifications as required by this section. When a minimum Indicator Score is required, it must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be met or exceeded for the Mortgage to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Borrowers without usable Credit Scores, manually underwriting without FICO scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: and analyzing all information contained in each of the Borrower’s credit reports and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: verifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Determining whether Credit Scores are usable
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine that each Credit Score (FICO score) received is usable. For a FICO
elements.label: text

elements.text: score to be usable, it must be based on sufficient, accurate information. Too little information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or information that is significantly inaccurate, makes the FICO score unusable for Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting. This is important both to ensure that the FICO score is adequately indicative of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower’s credit reputation and to ensure fairness for Borrowers in using Credit Scores to
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate their overall credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Insufficient information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although FICO scores may be generated if a consumer reporting agency (CRA)’s file
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes only one Tradeline, the Seller must not use any FICO score based on fewer than
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Tradelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Because a merged credit report or Residential Mortgage Credit Report may show more
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tradelines than were included in the particular CRA’s file used to generate the FICO
elements.label: text

elements.text: score, the Seller should request that the credit reporting company (CRC) indicate on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report the number of Tradelines that were used to create each FICO score.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatively, the Seller may obtain the in-file report used to create each FICO score and
elements.label: text

elements.text: use the in-file report to determine if the FICO score was based on at least three Tradelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B)Inaccurate information
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the CRC reports that the CRA file used to create a FICO score contains inaccurate
elements.label: text

elements.text: information about a Borrower’s credit history, the Seller must determine if the inaccuracy
elements.label: text

elements.text: is significant, and, if so, it must disregard that FICO score and explain the decision on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1077 or another document in the Mortgage file. However, minor discrepancies in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: balances owed or payment amounts on open accounts belonging to the Borrower are not to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be considered significant. FICO scores based on information that includes minor
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancies must not be disregarded.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must disregard FICO scores based on significant inaccuracies. Significant
elements.label: text

elements.text: inaccuracies include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Public records information on a bankruptcy, foreclosure, judgment or collection that
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not belong to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquencies that are reported in error
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One or more Tradelines that do not belong to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tradelines for accounts for which the Borrower is not the primary account holder but
elements.label: text

elements.text: is listed as an authorized user (authorized user accounts). However, the Seller does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: have to disregard the FICO score if the Seller obtains and retains evidence in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file of at least one of the following for each authorized user account:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Another Borrower on the Mortgage owns the Tradeline in question
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Tradeline is owned by the Borrower’s spouse, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower has been making the payments on the account for the last 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the monthly payment, as reported on the Borrower’s credit report, is included
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the monthly DTI ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller is unable to document one of the above three requirements for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized user account, the FICO score does not have to be disregarded if the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that the authorized user accounts have an insignificant impact on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s overall credit history and the information on the credit report is
elements.label: text

elements.text: representative of the Borrower’s own credit reputation. The Seller should base its
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination on the number of the Borrower’s own Tradelines as well as their age,
elements.label: text

elements.text: type, size and the payment history, as compared to the authorized user accounts. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must document its determination on Form 1077 or another document in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must not adjust the value of a FICO score because some information used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: create the score is inaccurate. The Seller may obtain and use a different FICO score from
elements.label: text

elements.text: another CRA if the score obtained from that CRA was not based on similar inaccurate
elements.label: text

elements.text: information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage that the Seller has determined the inaccurate information is significant,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file must contain written documentation from the CRA or the creditor
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting the inaccurate information affirming the errors. In addition, the Seller is strongly
elements.label: text

elements.text: encouraged to inform the Borrower that, pursuant to rights granted under the federal Fair
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Reporting Act, the Borrower has a right to contact both the CRA from which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: inaccurate FICO score was obtained and the furnisher of the inaccurate credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: to require the disputed credit information to be reinvestigated and corrected. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this paragraph do not apply to authorized user accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Underwriting with usable credit scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must comply with the following requirements when the Borrower has usable Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scores.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Identifying and using the Underwriting Score for each Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: To use Credit Scores to underwrite the Borrower’s credit reputation, the Seller must select
elements.label: text

elements.text: a single FICO score for each qualifying Borrower from the FICO scores that were
elements.label: text

elements.text: received and determined to be usable as described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To identify the Underwriting Score, the Seller must use the middle/lower method:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 2 The lower of the two FICO scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1 The FICO score
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the FICO scores are 660, 656 and 640, the single FICO score selected by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller would be 656. When there is a duplicate score, the Seller would select that score to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be the Underwriting Score. If the FICO scores for a Borrower are 660, 660 and 640, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller would select 660.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwriting Scores are used to identify an Indicator Score for the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B)Identifying and using the Indicator Score for the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Borrowers with usable Credit Scores, Freddie Mac requires that an Indicator Score be
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified and delivered for a Mortgage for reasons such as eligibility or pricing. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Indicator Score must be identified in accordance with any one of the three methods
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in the table below and must be delivered in accordance with the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5202.1(f). Freddie Mac prefers the Seller use one method for all of their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac recommends the Seller use the middle/lower then lowest method to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: an Indicator Score, as it is the most predictive of the overall credit reputation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before the Seller can identify an Indicator Score, the Seller must identify the Underwriting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score for each qualifying Borrower as described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower has no usable FICO score, and no Underwriting Score can be identified for
elements.label: text

elements.text: that Borrower, the Seller may use the Underwriting Scores for the remaining Borrowers to
elements.label: text

elements.text: identify the Indicator Score.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwriting Score is the Indicator Score for the Mortgage.

elements.label: text

elements.text: calculated from all usable FICO scores received for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum Indicator Score requirements for Mortgages are included in the individual
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide chapters for the product or offerings and in Exhibit 25, Mortgages with Risk Class
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or Minimum Indicator Score Requirements, and must be met or exceeded for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac. Sellers should also review other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents for additional Indicator Score requirements. For some Mortgages, a minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Indicator Score is required to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac. If no Borrower has a
elements.label: text

elements.text: usable FICO score and an Indicator Score is required for the Mortgage to be eligible, there
elements.label: text

elements.text: can be no Indicator Score for the Mortgage, and it is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Indicator Score does not indicate that the Borrower’s credit reputation is acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even when the Indicator Score for the Mortgage exceeds the minimum required, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must determine that each Borrower individually, and that all Borrowers collectively, have
elements.label: text

elements.text: an acceptable credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Additional requirements for Borrower’s with usable Credit Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: Because FICO scores reflect the information in the Borrower’s CRA credit file at the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: the FICO score was created, a Seller underwriting with FICO scores will find much of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation of the Borrower’s credit reputation is already reflected in the FICO score and
elements.label: text

elements.text: accompanying reason codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must use reason codes to identify credit factors that need to be addressed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining the Borrower has an acceptable credit reputation. The reason codes indicate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the most important reasons why a FICO score is not higher. The Seller must document
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit-related offsets for the risks indicated by the reason codes; for example, if the reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: codes indicate nonpayment of obligations, the Seller may establish that the Borrower was
elements.label: text

elements.text: unable to meet credit obligations because they experienced financial difficulties
elements.label: text

elements.text: attributable to extenuating circumstances. (See Section 5202.1(d) for more information
elements.label: text

elements.text: about adverse or derogatory credit information.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must not use the following factors as offsets for weaknesses in the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation because they have already been considered in creating the FICO score:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The absence or age of derogatory information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The number/proportion of accounts paid as agreed versus delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The types of accounts paid as agreed versus the types of accounts that are delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Recent pay-down or consolidation of account balances by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The length of the Borrower’s credit history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any combinations of the above factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Borrowers without usable Credit Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower doesn’t have a usable Credit Score, the Mortgage may be manually
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwritten using Noncredit Payment References, as described in this chapter, when it is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction or “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a 1- to 4-unit Primary Residence, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not a super conforming Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Additional requirements for evaluating Borrower credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: An acceptable credit reputation is established by a history that, when viewed as a whole,
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidences a Borrower’s willingness to make ongoing payments and ability to manage
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations as agreed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine that each Borrower individually, and that all Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: collectively, have acceptable credit reputations. The acceptable credit reputation of one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower cannot be used to offset the unacceptable credit reputation of another.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When evaluating each Borrower’s credit reputation, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review all of the credit documentation in the Mortgage file, including the credit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: and direct verifications, to determine the data is accurate and the documentation in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file is consistent with the credit report;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Thoroughly review and evaluate the Borrower’s entire credit history, in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of this section, with the exception of trended credit data, which must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be considered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Trended credit data is expanded credit information reflecting historical Tradeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: data such as balances, scheduled payments and actual payments reported for each month
elements.label: text

elements.text: over an extended period of time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate the Borrower(s) overall credit reputation using the factors described in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section and those listed in the reason codes accompanying the FICO score, for Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: with usable Credit Scores;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify and document credit-related offsets for significant derogatory information
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the credit history, such as bankruptcy, foreclosure, short sale or recent late
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing payments (see Section 5202.1(d));
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ After credit reputation is established, evaluate the overall layering of risk (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5102.2(b)); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document its evaluation and conclusion that the credit reputation is acceptable on Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1077 or on a separate document in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: In evaluating the Borrower’s credit reputation, the Seller must weigh the following factors in
elements.label: text

elements.text: arriving at a conclusion that the Borrower’s credit reputation is acceptable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The type and amount of credit outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. How long the Borrower has had credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. How the Borrower uses available credit, including revolving balances-to-limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Recent changes in the number of open accounts or overall amount of credit outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The payment history and status of all accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Any recent inquiries shown on the credit report
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Any public record or collection items
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower’s credit history includes both Tradelines and Noncredit Payment References,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must put more weight on the Tradelines when evaluating the Borrower’s credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation and cannot use Noncredit Payment References to offset derogatory credit in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tradeline reference. If a Borrower’s credit history includes housing obligations (rental or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage), the Seller should put more weight on how housing payments were made than non￾housing payments but must not ignore any derogatory information in the credit history.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must also consider layering of risk in its evaluation of credit reputation. A stronger
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation may be required if either capacity or collateral is weak. (See Sections 5102.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 5102.2(b) for more information on how to evaluate the overall risk of the Mortgage using
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation, capacity and collateral.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Adverse or derogatory credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adverse credit information in and of itself does not mean the Borrower’s credit reputation is
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable. When there is adverse or derogatory information in the Borrower’s credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: history, the Seller must establish whether the derogatory information is significant.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s credit history includes significant adverse or derogatory credit within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent two years, the Borrower’s credit reputation must not be considered acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other significant derogatory information, the Seller must document extenuating
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances or conclude that it was due to financial mismanagement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Establishing whether adverse or derogatory credit information is significant
elements.label: text

elements.text: Derogatory information must always be considered significant if any of the following exist:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is underwritten using FICO scores and at least two reason codes are related
elements.label: text

elements.text: to nonpayment of obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are several accounts showing recent late payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are multiple 60- or 90-day late payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is more than one 30-day late housing payment in the last 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are more than two 30-day or more than one 60-day late housing payments within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent two years
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The number and size of the delinquent accounts are large in relation to the overall credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are multiple episodes of late payments extending over a period of time
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The credit history shows derogatory credit information within the two most recent years
elements.label: text

elements.text: combined with multiple revolving accounts with high balances-to-limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The public record information reveals several occurrences of derogatory credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, including judgments, tax liens and/or non-medical collection accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is a bankruptcy, foreclosure, deed-in-lieu of foreclosure or short sale within the last
elements.label: text

elements.text: seven years that is disclosed on a credit report, disclosed by the Borrower on Form 65 or is
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidenced by other documentation contained in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: When establishing whether other adverse or derogatory credit information is significant, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must weigh the amount of derogatory information against the rest of the credit history
elements.label: text

elements.text: and determine whether it is significant. When making this determination, the Seller should not
elements.label: text

elements.text: ignore any derogatory credit but must give more weight to late housing payments and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: derogatory information or late payments occurring within the past two years. Generally, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: more recent the adverse or derogatory credit information, the more likely it is significant.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must consider all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The number, timing and extent of the adverse or derogatory credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The number, type and size of accounts with adverse or derogatory credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Public record information, such as judgments and non-medical collection accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other characteristics listed in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A 30-day late housing payment has more weight than a 30-day late non-housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, and a non-medical collection account has more weight than a 30-day late payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a revolving account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although there may be many situations involving derogatory credit information that are less
elements.label: text

elements.text: clear, especially when disputes about obligations are involved, the derogatory credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: information is not significant when it consists only of isolated late payments, even if several
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts show sporadic late payments, provided all of the following exist:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The late payments were not recent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The late payments did not extend beyond one month
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The number and size of delinquent accounts is not large in relation to the overall credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The credit history does not show multiple revolving accounts with high balances-to-limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: or high overall utilization of revolving credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All other credit has been paid as agreed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Timeshare loans are considered installment debts, regardless of how they are reported on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s credit report; therefore, payments on timeshare loans are not considered housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments for purposes of evaluating late housing payments as required above. Additionally,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure recovery time periods for reestablishment of credit below do not apply to
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeshare loans in foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller determines that the derogatory information is not significant, it must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation supporting its conclusion in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Evaluating significant adverse or derogatory information caused by extenuating
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac considers an extenuating circumstance to be a nonrecurring or isolated
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstance or set of circumstances that was beyond the Borrower’s control and that
elements.label: text

elements.text: significantly reduced income and/or increased expenses and rendered the Borrower unable
elements.label: text

elements.text: to repay obligations as agreed, resulting in significant adverse or derogatory credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s credit history includes significant adverse or derogatory credit within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: most recent two years, even if it was caused by extenuating circumstances, the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation cannot be considered acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller uses extenuating circumstances to justify that the Borrower’s credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation is acceptable despite significant adverse or derogatory information, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must use third-party documentation to confirm the extenuating circumstances and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has reestablished an acceptable credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller determines that the Borrower’s credit reputation is acceptable despite
elements.label: text

elements.text: significant adverse or derogatory information caused by extenuating circumstances, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file must contain all of the following documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A written statement from the Borrower, in the form of a signed letter or an e-mail
elements.label: text

elements.text: directly from the Borrower, attributing the cause of the financial difficulties to outside
elements.label: text

elements.text: factors beyond the Borrower’s control that are not ongoing and are unlikely to recur
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Third-party documentation confirming that the events related by the Borrower in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation were an isolated occurrence and significantly reduced the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: income and/or increased expenses and rendered the Borrower unable to repay as
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An underwriting analysis on Form 1077 or on a separate document in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file, relating the Borrower’s explanation to the Mortgage file documentation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: leading to a reasonable conclusion that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The explanation is consistent with the adverse information reported and the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: information in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The events causing the financial difficulties were beyond the Borrower’s control,
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not ongoing and are unlikely to recur; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower has reestablished an acceptable credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence on the credit report and other documentation in the Mortgage file of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: length of time since completion of the significant derogatory event to the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: application (i.e., the recovery time period)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller is unable to document extenuating circumstances in accordance with

elements.label: text

elements.text: Making a case that the Borrower is sufficiently willing to repay obligations when

elements.label: text

elements.label: text
