elements.text: (ii) Documenting Borrower explanation of adverse or derogatory information
elements.label: text

elements.text: A written explanation is required for significant derogatory information. The purpose for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requiring a written explanation is to assist the Seller in determining whether the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit problems were due to extenuating circumstances (factors clearly beyond the control of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower) or whether they reflect financial mismanagement (the Borrower’s disregard for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payment of obligations when due).
elements.label: text

elements.text: When adverse or derogatory information is not significant, the Seller may need to request that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower provide a written explanation, in the form of a signed letter or an e-mail directly
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Borrower, of the circumstances causing the payment difficulty. The decision to
elements.label: text

elements.text: require an explanation letter should be based on such factors as the age of the delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: account, the frequency and severity of late payments, the size of the account balance and
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, when the late payments occurred and the status of the Borrower’s other credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to accomplish this purpose, it may be necessary to allow someone to assist the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower in preparing the explanation. If the Borrower needs assistance in preparing a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation, another party, such as the real estate agent or loan officer, should be encouraged
elements.label: text

elements.text: to assist the Borrower in preparing the explanation. As long as the explanation accurately
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflects the facts as related by the Borrower (as evidenced by the Borrower’s signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: attesting accuracy), the Seller should accept the explanation as documentation for its review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A written explanation in and of itself does not satisfy the Seller’s responsibility to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s willingness to repay. When adverse or derogatory information is considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: significant, as explained in Section 5202.1(d), the Seller must relate the reasons for the late
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, as stated by the Borrower, to the other information about the Borrower’s credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: history contained in the Mortgage file. The Seller must reasonably be able to conclude that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The explanation is consistent with the adverse information reported and the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: information in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The explanation establishes a credible cause for the late payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower represents an acceptable credit risk and exhibits the ability and willingness
elements.label: text

elements.text: to repay the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Evaluating other credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: Inquiries on the credit report generally reflect the Borrower’s requests for new or additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the credit report indicates that a creditor has made an inquiry within the previous
elements.label: text

elements.text: 90-day period, the Seller must determine if additional credit was granted. If additional credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: was granted, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain verification of the debt; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include the monthly payment in the DTI ratio calculation. Refer to Section 5401.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When underwriting with FICO scores, a reason code will alert the Seller that the number of
elements.label: text

elements.text: inquiries affected the Borrower’s FICO score and, therefore, should not be overlooked in
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting. In this case and when underwriting without FICO scores, the Seller must decide
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the number of recent inquiries, especially when combined with other credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, increases the risk of the Borrower’s credit profile.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Several inquiries within the most recent 12 months generally increase risk and, when
elements.label: text

elements.text: combined with high balances-to-limits on revolving accounts, may indicate that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: is in danger of becoming overextended. In addition, several recent inquiries combined with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit history of short duration may make even mild derogatory credit information significant.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To address how risk evidenced by several recent inquiries, layered with other credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: risks, affects the Borrower’s overall credit reputation, the Seller must look at:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The age of the Borrower’s other credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The type of credit being sought
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total amount of credit outstanding, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The overall credit utilization reflected on the report
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Age of accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must review the age of the Borrower’s credit obligations to determine whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: there has been a recent, significant increase in the number of open accounts. The age of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: account is found on a credit report by referring to the “date opened” column. The length of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s credit history can be measured from the oldest account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Like inquiries, several recently opened accounts may be a warning that the Borrower could
elements.label: text

elements.text: become overextended and require a more conservative approach to reviewing both Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation and capacity. A credit history with all recently opened accounts may indicate
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Borrower lacks sufficient experience managing financial obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller should also review the age of accounts to determine if there has been a significant
elements.label: text

elements.text: change in the Borrower’s credit profile. A change in the Borrower’s pattern of credit use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which includes several newly opened revolving accounts, several inquiries and high
elements.label: text

elements.text: utilization of revolving Tradelines, introduces significant layering of risk to the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When underwriting with FICO scores, a reason code will alert the Seller that the age of
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts affected the Borrower’s FICO score and therefore should not be overlooked in
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting. In this case and when underwriting without FICO scores, the Seller must decide
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the age of accounts, combined with other credit information, increases the risk of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s profile.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To address how the age of a Borrower’s accounts, layered with other credit risks, affects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s credit reputation, the Seller must consider:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of outstanding credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The overall utilization of revolving accounts, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Recent inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Balances-to-limits/high overall utilization of revolving credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must evaluate balances-to-limits ratios of revolving accounts and the overall
elements.label: text

elements.text: revolving credit utilization.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When evaluating balances-to-limits, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compare the current balance for each open account to the high credit or limit to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether there is a pattern of accounts with balances at or near their limits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consider multiple revolving accounts with balances more than 50% of their limits an
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional risk when evaluating credit reputation. The more accounts with high balances￾to-limits and the higher the percentage used, the higher the risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: High balances-to-limits may also indicate the Borrower is making minimum payments on
elements.label: text

elements.text: revolving accounts rather than reducing the debt and may be at or near payment capacity. Any
elements.label: text

elements.text: derogatory information in a credit history within the most recent two years combined with
elements.label: text

elements.text: several revolving accounts at or near their limits should be considered significant derogatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: information when evaluating the credit reputation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When evaluating revolving credit utilization, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compare the overall amount of outstanding revolving credit to the overall amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: revolving credit available to the Borrower, as shown on the credit report, to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit utilization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consider usage of more than 60% of available revolving credit a risk factor when
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating credit reputation. The higher the Borrower’s overall utilization of revolving
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit, the higher the risk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A pattern of revolving accounts at or near their limits and utilization of a high proportion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the overall revolving credit available to the Borrower, especially when combined with newly
elements.label: text

elements.text: opened accounts, indicates that the Borrower is becoming overextended and there is
elements.label: text

elements.text: significant risk in the Borrower’s credit reputation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: For a Caution Mortgage with at least two Feedback Certificate messages related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: high balances-to-limits or high overall utilization of revolving credit, the Seller should
elements.label: text

elements.text: presume the Borrower’s credit reputation is unacceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may not use the lack of adverse or derogatory credit information as an offset for
elements.label: text

elements.text: high balances-to-limits or high overall utilization of revolving credit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When underwriting with FICO scores, a reason code will alert the Seller that the balances on
elements.label: text

elements.text: revolving accounts are too high or the proportion of balance to high credit on bank revolving
elements.label: text

elements.text: or all revolving accounts is too high and affected the Borrower’s FICO score.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine whether the balances-to-limits and overall revolving utilization,
elements.label: text

elements.text: combined with other credit information, make the Borrower’s credit reputation unacceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A Borrower with multiple revolving accounts with balances at or near limits,
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall utilization of revolving credit of 90%, and less than four years of credit history would
elements.label: text

elements.text: have an unacceptable credit reputation, even if there were no derogatory information in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report, unless the Borrower had sufficient cash reserves to pay off all revolving account
elements.label: text

elements.text: balances and the Borrower’s total DTI ratio was within guidelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To address how high balances-to-limits, when layered with other credit risks, affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s overall credit reputation, the Seller should look at all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The age of the Borrower’s credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The number of accounts with outstanding balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Recent inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total amount of credit outstanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Documenting and delivering Underwriting Scores and Indicator Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Documenting the Underwriting Score and the Indicator Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Minimum Indicator Score is required, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note on Form 1077 or a similar document in the Mortgage file the Underwriting Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Indicator Score and how each was identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retain the source documentation for the Indicator Score in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Delivering an Indicator Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Indicator Score is based on usable Credit Scores in accordance with this section, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must deliver the Indicator Score in ULDD Data Point Loan Level Credit Score Value
elements.label: text

elements.text: and deliver the method used by the Seller to determine the Indicator Score in ULDD Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Point Loan Level Credit Score Selection Method Type:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “Middle Or Lower Then Lowest”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “Middle or Lower Then Average”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “Average Then Average”
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller determines there is no usable Indicator Score in the Mortgage file due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: insufficient credit information, the Seller must select the valid value of “Insufficient Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: History” in ULDD Data Point Credit Score Impairment Type.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller determines there is no usable Indicator Score in the Mortgage file due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: significant inaccurate credit information, the Seller must select the valid value of
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Significant Errors Score” in ULDD Data Point Credit Score Impairment Type.

elements.text: Chapter 5203: Credit Reports and Credit Scores
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5203.1: Credit reports (04/22/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit report requirements for Loan Product Advisor® Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit report requirements for Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit report requirements for all Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit reports are used to evaluate the Borrower’s creditworthiness, including the calculation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: total monthly debt payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manually Underwritten Mortgages and Loan Product Advisor Mortgages, each Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted to Freddie Mac must contain at least one written credit report that meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Additional terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following additional terms are used in this chapter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consumer reporting agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (CRA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A consumer reporting agency (CRA) is an organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: engaged primarily in gathering, recording, updating,
elements.label: text

elements.text: storing and distributing financial and public record
elements.label: text

elements.text: information concerning the debt repayment histories of
elements.label: text

elements.text: individuals being considered for credit extension. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit reporting company
elements.label: text

elements.text: (CRC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A credit reporting company (CRC), also known as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit reporting agency reseller, is an organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: engaged in the preparation and sale of credit reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These reports contain data obtained from CRAs and may
elements.label: text

elements.text: also contain information and verifications obtained from
elements.label: text

elements.text: other sources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: I
elements.label: text

elements.text: In-file credit reports An in-file credit report contains “as is” information from
elements.label: text

elements.text: a CRA that has not been updated or re-verified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Manually Underwritten Mortgage, if the Seller uses
elements.label: text

elements.text: in-file credit reports, the Seller must obtain reports from
elements.label: text

elements.text: at least two CRAs for each Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: M
elements.label: text

elements.text: Merged credit reports A merged credit report includes the credit information
elements.label: text

elements.text: from multiple CRAs for an individual Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A joint merged credit report includes the CRA credit data
elements.label: text

elements.text: for two individual Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The credit information from each CRA may be presented
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a stacked merged credit report (i.e., all records from all
elements.label: text

elements.text: CRAs are included in the report), or the CRC may
elements.label: text

elements.text: eliminate duplicate records through an automated merge
elements.label: text

elements.text: process. Each CRC may use a slightly different merge
elements.label: text

elements.text: logic to eliminate duplicate records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Mortgage Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report (RMCR)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) is a
elements.label: text

elements.text: detailed account, prepared by a CRC, of the credit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment and residence history, as well as public
elements.label: text

elements.text: records information, for an individual Borrower or two
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit information from multiple CRAs is merged and
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified by a CRC before the RMCR is sent to the user.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The CRC may verify other information not included in
elements.label: text

elements.text: CRA records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: T
elements.label: text

elements.text: Trended credit data Trended credit data is expanded credit information that
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflects historical Tradeline data, such as balances,
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled payments and actual payments, reported for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each month over an extended period of time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Credit report requirements for Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac encourages the Seller to use the merged credit reports or joint merged credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports obtained through Loan Product Advisor; however, the Seller may use credit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained outside of Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit reports used in connection with Loan Product Advisor Mortgages must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit reports submitted to Loan Product Advisor must include trended credit data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All credit reports must be of the same type. For example, the Seller may not use a merged
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report for one Borrower and an RMCR for another Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All credit reports used by the Seller must be included in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the credit report is obtained outside of Loan Product Advisor, the Seller must request the
elements.label: text

elements.text: CRA or CRC to include FICO scores, or VantageScore 4.0 scores if the Seller is approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: for their use as required by Section 5203.2(c), from all credit repositories on all credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor will automatically request FICO® scores for each Borrower for credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports obtained through Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Credit report requirements for Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit reports used in connection with Non-Loan Product Advisor Mortgages must meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All credit reports must be of the same type. For example, the Seller may not use a merged
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit report for one Borrower and an RMCR for another Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All in-file credit reports for any one Borrower must be dated within 14 days of each other
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All credit reports used by the Seller must be included in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is not submitted to Loan Product Advisor, the Seller must request the CRA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or CRC to include FICO scores from all credit repositories on all credit reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5203.2 for additional information on Credit Scores.

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Glossary terms used in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum number of Credit Scores
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Score requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assessment of Credit Fees for Mortgages with certain Indicator Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Glossary terms used in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller should be familiar with the Glossary definitions of the following terms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score A number summarizing an individual’s credit profile that
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicates the likelihood that a Borrower will repay future
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Indicator Score The one Underwriting Score identified to represent the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility of the Mortgage for the product offering. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: related ULDD Data Point names are Loan Level Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Score Selection Method Type and Loan Level Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: Value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: U
elements.label: text

elements.text: Underwriting Score The one Credit Score selected from all usable Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Scores obtained for an individual Borrower that quantifies
elements.label: text

elements.text: the credit reputation risk for that individual Borrower. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: related ULDD Data Point name is Credit Score Value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Minimum number of Credit Scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must request Credit Scores and accompanying reason codes for each Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: from at least two of the consumer reporting agencies (CRAs). Credit Scores can be obtained
elements.label: text

elements.text: by requesting a credit reporting company (CRC) to provide them as part of the credit report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: It is unusual for a Borrower who reports credit obligations on the application not to have a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score. If no Credit Score is received for such a Borrower, the Seller must re-check the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information provided when ordering the Credit Scores and resubmit a request if an error is
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Required Credit Score versions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac is transitioning toward a modernized and more competitive Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: framework for Mortgage underwriting. In support of this transition, the U.S. Federal Housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (FHFA) has approved the use of Classic FICO®, VantageScore® 4.0 and FICO Score 10T
elements.label: text

elements.text: models.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each of the three major U.S. CRAs uses a different name to refer to Classic FICO scores;
elements.label: text

elements.text: however, all FICO scores are based on the same underlying FICO scoring model developed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Fair Isaac Corporation. The following Credit Score versions generated by each CRA are
elements.label: text

elements.text: used to determine the Underwriting Score and Indicator Score for Mortgages delivered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Equifax® FICO Classic v5
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Experian®/Fair Isaac Risk Model v2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ TransUnion® FICO Risk Score 04
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following versions of VantageScore 4.0 are also eligible for use by approved Sellers:
elements.label: text

elements.text: VantageScore 4.0 versions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Equifax VantageScore 4.0
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Experian VantageScore 4.0
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ TransUnion VantageScore 4.0
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers that wish to participate in the delivery of Mortgages assessed using VantageScore 4.0
elements.label: text

elements.text: should contact their Freddie Mac representative or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding program availability, eligibility requirements and applicable delivery

elements.text: 5301.1: General requirements for all stable monthly
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information and guidance related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview of stable monthly income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements for all stable monthly income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income stability and history requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income continuance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Alimony payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview of stable monthly income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic 5300 provides requirements and guidance for the determination of stable monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: income. The Seller must determine when additional analysis and documentation is needed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the determination of stable and consistent monthly income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General requirements for all stable monthly income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Stable monthly income: Stable monthly income is the Borrower’s verified gross monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: income from all acceptable and verifiable sources that can reasonably be expected to
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue for at least the next three years. For each income source used to qualify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, the Seller must determine that both the source and the amount of the income are
elements.label: text

elements.text: stable, with a consistent level of earnings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income used to qualify the Borrower (whether or not specifically addressed as an income
elements.label: text

elements.text: source or type in Topic 5300) and the documentation in the Mortgage file must be evaluated
elements.label: text

elements.text: for stable monthly income qualification requirements and must meet the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic 5300.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income that does not meet these requirements or is not calculated correctly may invalidate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Loan Product Advisor® Risk Class on the Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cryptocurrency: Income that is paid to the Borrower in cryptocurrency may not be used for
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file documentation: All documentation used to establish stable monthly income
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Income stability and history requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must consider the length of history of the income and whether the earnings have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been consistent. When evaluating stability of income based upon historical receipt, additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: layering of risk may be present depending upon the degree of income fluctuation. As a result,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must determine when additional documentation (e.g., an additional year of
elements.label: text

elements.text: earnings history) is necessary to support income stability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In most instances, a two-year history of receiving a consistent level of income is required for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the income to be considered stable and used for qualifying. While the source of income may
elements.label: text

elements.text: vary, the Borrower must have a consistent level of income despite changes in the sources of
elements.label: text

elements.text: income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Income continuance requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all income used to qualify the Borrower, the Seller must determine whether the income is
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonably expected to continue. This determination must focus on the Borrower’s past
elements.label: text

elements.text: employment/self-employment history, history of receipt of other income and the probability
elements.label: text

elements.text: of continued consistent receipt of the income used to qualify the Borrower. At a minimum,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must base the determination on the requirements of Topic 5300 and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation contained in the Mortgage file. Additional documentation may be required, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 5302.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must not consider income for qualifying the Borrower if the Seller has knowledge,
elements.label: text

elements.text: information or documentation that contradicts a reasonable expectation of continuance or
elements.label: text

elements.text: probability of consistent receipt over at least the next three years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5302.1: Introduction to documentation requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For all income and each qualification source used to qualify the Borrower, the Seller must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: the required documentation and verifications listed in the respective income sections of Topic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional documentation may be necessary to evaluate, justify and explain the qualification of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower. This includes scenarios in which the Seller has knowledge that the documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: originally provided by the Borrower may not be reflective of the Borrower’s current level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, even though the documentation meets the age of documentation requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A more recent paystub may be necessary if, after the original paystub was provided,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s industry was impacted by economic conditions or widespread business closures
elements.label: text

elements.text: that could affect hours worked and income received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All documentation must be maintained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation.
